content: Just as the linguistic definition of reflexion frames it as both a light/information reflection off a surface and a deliberate act of metacognitive reconsideration, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to split their function into two complementary stages: passive 'reflection' of inconsistent solution perspectives (as seen in Self-Contrast) and active 'reflexion' (intentional metacognitive review) of those reflected inconsistencies to refine outputs. This dual framing turns the overlapping linguistic and agentive meanings of reflexion into a actionable framework for improving LLM self-reflection.
